---
title: "*Dry Bulbs, Wet Bulbs, and My Will to Live: Mastering the Psych Chart* (Psychrometric Charts) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *Dry Bulbs, Wet Bulbs, and My Will to Live: Mastering the Psych Chart*

Main title explanation

## Dry Bulbs, Wet Bulbs, and My Will to Live: Mastering the Psych Chart

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/Nz8ADo4X2Ss?si=1pZTmEr-0mScXDlk" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

[Dry Bulb Temperature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dry-bulb_temperature)
: The temperature of an air-water vapor mixture as reported by a regular thermometer.

[Wet Bulb Temperature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wet-bulb_temperature)
: The temperature of an air-water vapor mixture as reported by a thermometer that is covered by a moist porous covering.

[Dew Point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dew_point)
: The temperature at which moisture begins to condense out of an air-water vapor mixture

[Relative Humidity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidity#Relative_humidity)
: The partial pressure of water in air-water vapor mixture divided by the saturation pressure at the same temperature.

[Absolute Humidity or Humidity Ratio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidity#Specific_humidity)
: The mass of water vapor per mass of dry air in an air-water vapor mixture.

Specific Enthalpy
: The enthalpy per unit mass. In psychrometry it is reported as the enthalpy of moist air per kilogram of dry air.

Specific Volume
: The volume per unit mass. In psychrometry it is reported as the volume of moist air per kilogram of dry air.

[Sling Psychrometer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hygrometer#Sling_psychrometer)
: A device with a dry-bulb thermometer and a wet-bulb thermometer that is spun or slung through an air-water vapor mixture to measure thedry bulb and wet bulb temperatures.

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**The Enthalpy Games: Sunrise on the Calculations**<br/>*Example for $\mathrm{N_2}$, $\Delta H$ from $C_p$, $\Delta U$ from $C_v$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/yLJp3YQaVvo), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/HeatCapacityVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/94-EnthalpyVsTandPPart2.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Feeling the Heat? How to Get Your Best Estimate**<br/>*Estimating $C_p$, $\Delta \hat{H}_v$, $\Delta \hat{H}_m$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/f_l3OFyuiPs), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/EstimatingHeatCapacitiesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/95-EstimatingHeatCapacitiesVisuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**Reference Not Found: Mixing, Dissolving, and Changing the Rules**<br/>*$\Delta H_m$, $\Delta H_s$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/ggU692PLWts), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/HeatsOfMixingVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/98-ReferenceStatesVIsuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**The Isochronicles: This First Law Saga Is a Gas**<br/>*The 1^st^ Law for Ideal Gases for Isothermal, Isobaric, Isochoric, and Isentropic processes*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/iKsR3v1wu30), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/The1stLawForIdealGases.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/102-1stLawIdealGasesVisuals.qmd)
